<onlyinclude><templatestyles src="Infobox_enemy/styles.css" /><infobox theme="enemy-infobox">
    <title source="name">
        <format><center>'''{{{name}}}'''{{#if: {{{enemy_type|}}} | <br/><span class="enemy-type-{{{enemy_type}}}" style="font-size: 0.7em; font-weight: normal;">{{{enemy_type}}}</span>}}</format>
    </title>
    <image source="icon">
        <format>[[File:{{{icon}}}|center|alt=Icon of {{{name}}}]]</format>
    </image>
    <panel>
        <data source="tags">
            <label>Tags</label>
            <format>{{{tags}}}</format>
        </data>
        <data source="can_be_despawned">
            <label>Despawnable</label>
            <format>{{{can_be_despawned}}}</format>
        </data>{{#if: {{{api_name|}}} |
        <section collapse="closed">
            <header>Technical Information</header>
            <data source="api_name">
                <label>API Name</label>
                <format><code>{{{api_name}}}</code></format>
            </data>
            <data source="internal_name">
                <label>Internal Name</label>
                <format><code>{{{internal_name}}}</code></format>
            </data>
        </section>}}
    </panel>
</infobox></onlyinclude>
==Documentation==
This template displays an enemy (hostile NPC) in a Portable Infobox.

==How to Use==
<pre>
{{Infobox enemy
|name = Display name (e.g. "Slicer")
|api_name = Internal APIName (e.g. "Slicer")
|internal_name = Internal class name (optional)
|enemy_type = Grunt | Brute | Abomination | Boss
|tags = Comma-separated behavior tags (e.g. "Gunner, Tall, Turret")
|can_be_despawned = Yes | No
|icon = Icon filename (optional — enemies have no dump icon yet)
}}
</pre>

==Example Usage==
<pre>
{{Infobox enemy
|name = Slicer
|api_name = Slicer
|enemy_type = Abomination
|tags = Gunner
|can_be_despawned = Yes
}}
</pre>
===Result===
{{Infobox enemy
|name = Slicer
|api_name = Slicer
|enemy_type = Abomination
|tags = Gunner
|can_be_despawned = Yes
}}

[[Category:Templates]]
